records.title: vendor A pricing and api note
records.text_summary: Vendor A notes. Pricing is usage-based pricing. Context window is 200k context. Integration path is an OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Noise includes old procurement questions and archived SSO notes.

records.title: vendor B enterprise note
records.text_summary: Vendor B notes. Pricing is flat enterprise plan with annual commitment. Context window is 128k context. API surface relies on a proprietary SDK. Noise includes old partner program chatter and security questionnaire backlog.

records.title: vendor C ingestion note
records.text_summary: Vendor C notes. Context window is 1M context. The platform exposes REST and batch ingestion APIs. Noise includes historical launch notes and unrelated benchmark screenshots.

records.title: vendor pricing facts
records.text_summary: Stable facts: Vendor A uses usage-based pricing. Vendor B uses a flat enterprise plan with annual commitment.

records.title: vendor context window facts
records.text_summary: Stable facts: Vendor A offers 200k context, Vendor B offers 128k context, and Vendor C offers 1M context.

records.title: vendor api facts
records.text_summary: Stable facts: Vendor A exposes an OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Vendor B depends on a proprietary SDK. Vendor C supports REST and batch ingestion.

records.title: vendor comparison topic
records.text_summary: Topic: Vendor A = usage-based pricing, 200k context, OpenAI-compatible endpoint; Vendor B = flat enterprise plan, annual commitment, 128k context, proprietary SDK; Vendor C = 1M context, REST and batch ingestion.

cases.query_text: Which vendor uses usage-based pricing and what context window does it provide?
Which vendor uses a flat enterprise plan with annual commitment and what API surface does it require?
Which vendor offers 1M context and what ingestion interfaces are available?
